channel.title: TabSyncBridge Updates
channel.description: Product, setup, and privacy-safe marketing updates for TabSyncBridge.

item.title: Why Browser Tab Sync Usually Uses Cloud Accounts
item.description: Why browser tab sync often uses cloud accounts, and how TabSyncBridge keeps Safari and Chromium tab sync on your private local network.

item.title: TabSyncBridge Support
item.description: Setup, troubleshooting, Restore Purchases, and Apple-managed trial and subscription help for TabSyncBridge.

item.title: Install TabSyncBridge
item.description: Start with the iPhone app, then add Safari or Chromium browser support for private local-network tab sync.

item.title: Private Local Tab Sync Guide
item.description: How TabSyncBridge keeps tab sync on your own private local network without a cloud account.

item.title: Chrome to iPhone Tab Sync Guide
item.description: Set up Chromium-family browser tab sync with iPhone using TabSyncBridge.

item.title: Desktop Bridge Setup Guide
item.description: What the desktop bridge does for Chromium-family browser workflows.

item.title: Safari and Chromium Tab Sync Guide
item.description: How iPhone Safari and desktop Chromium-family browsers work together with TabSyncBridge.

item.title: Close Remote Tabs Guide
item.description: How TabSyncBridge routes close-tab requests to the browser profile that owns the tab.

item.title: Sync Tabs Without a Cloud Account
item.description: How TabSyncBridge syncs browser tabs across iPhone, Safari, and Chromium-family browsers on your private local network without a cloud account.
